title: chezmoi secret integration
outcome: chezmoi secret references wired without storing any value. private_dot_config/wfos-secrets/env.sh.tmpl (mode 0600 via private_) references the pass vault via chezmoi's native {{ pass "wfos/sample" }} function, guarded on the profile `secrets` flag; the else branch resolves nothing. Double protection: the .config/wfos-secrets target is in the secrets category that .chezmoiignore.tmpl already excludes for agent-safe and headless-dev, so chezmoi diff for those profiles never renders the file and never resolves a reference. The file-oriented sops+age vault gets packages/panoply/secrets/: .sops.yaml creation rules (age recipient placeholder), a structure-only sample.config.yaml (fake placeholders), and a README with the deferred encryption recipe.
validation: validate-secrets.sh [3/5] PASS: secret template guards on $p.secrets, references the pass function, agent-safe excludes the secrets category, .chezmoiignore maps secrets -> .config/wfos-secrets, no secret-looking literals in templates/fixtures. validate.sh phase-5 confirms env.sh.tmpl delimiter/control balance. The secret template is static-checked only and never passed to chezmoi execute-template.
validation_deferred: Live `chezmoi diff` (agent-safe never resolves a secret) and `chezmoi apply` (local-macos-full resolves inline) are DEFERRED: chezmoi is not installed (dotfiles module owns install). Real sops+age encryption is DEFERRED until a human provisions an age recipient (no key material generated this session).
decisions: Per the protocol ('define the rails, do not exercise them') and the deferral precedent from E01, no secret is read or resolved and no age keypair is generated; the sops fixture ships rules + structure + recipe only
sops fixtures live outside the chezmoi source (packages/panoply/secrets/) so .sops.yaml is not mistaken for a $HOME render target
agent-safe diff safety is achieved two ways: category exclusion in .chezmoiignore.tmpl AND a $p.secrets template guard
token_note: loaded 1 story block + STATE/ledger tail; no canon ref needed
